5 POLICY STATEMENT

shall develop, implement, and maintain a Security Operations Policy ("Policy")  protection of assets through secure operational processes thereby contributing to an overall safe and secure organization.

3 PURPOSE

The purpose of this Policy is to protect data/Information and information processing facilities through secure and effective operations at .

6.2 Controls ver System Documentation

shall ensure that:

1. System documentation is kept under safe custody.
2. Access is restricted to only those who have a business need and are authorized.
3. Version control is implemented to ensure that the current version is consistently available.
4. Copying of any system documentation shall be controlled and shall be allowed only after authorization.
5. The respective custodians of documents shall hold the original set of the system documentation.

6.5 System Acceptance

shall ensure that:

1. System acceptance tests are done in an appropriate test environment.
2. Before moving any new system into production, it shall be tested as per the System Acceptance Plan.
2. ’s system performance requirements.
3. ’s Security requirements.
4. ’s business continuity requirements.
5. Error recovery and restart procedures.
6. Effects of the new system on other existing systems.
7. User training requirements and ease of use of new systems, so as to ensur

6.3 Separation of Development, Test and Operational Environments

shall ensure that:

1. Production environment shall be logically separated from test and development environments.
2. Access to each environment shall be controlled using firewalls.
3. Transfer of software between the development, test and production environments shall be subject to specific procedures such as the  Information Systems Acquisition, Development and Maintenance Procedure and will be documented.
